hyperedges.label: Tracking system across plan and implementation
CSP hardening across headers, meta, and hosting configs

nodes.label: CI merge gate (pnpm check)
nodes.rationale: Delivery harness F3: the same pnpm check that runs locally is the merge gate — trust lives in the gate, not the person or model. Runs on PRs and non-master/main pushes, pinning Node via .nvmrc and frozen-lockfile install.

nodes.label: v2.3.0 MVP release
nodes.rationale: MVP shipped to Cloudflare Pages: missing site pages, real tracking portal wired to hit-ever2, first brand color system, delivery harness, and GTM install.

nodes.label: Real tracking portal wired to hit-ever2
nodes.rationale: TrackingPortal.tsx fetches live shipment data from the hit-ever2 worker (GET /track/:id, primary by waybill, fallback by carrier number) with loading/ok/not-found/error states, replacing the previous mock; honest coming-soon fallback only when PUBLIC_API_URL is explicitly disabled.
nodes.norm_label: real tracking portal wired to hit-ever2

nodes.label: 4-milestone standardized journey modal
nodes.rationale: Provider event logs differ between Everest and Global Connection, so raw logs are mapped to a fixed 4-milestone journey (In Miami warehouse -> In transit -> In Nicaragua -> Delivered) shown in a modal, with the current milestone bolded and dated only where reliable.

nodes.label: Naranja HIT brand palette rebrand
nodes.rationale: Protagonist color moved from red #FF3B3F to Naranja HIT #FF7A00 (with a 50-800 scale) plus Azul Navy #14213D support; the old amber accent retired so orange stays scarce per the 70/20/10 proportion.

nodes.label: Floating WhatsApp widget
nodes.rationale: Bottom-right button on every page opening wa.me with a prefilled greeting; emits a whatsapp_click dataLayer event so a GTM pixel / GA conversion can be wired with no code change.

nodes.label: track_search / whatsapp_click dataLayer events
nodes.rationale: The site code emits dataLayer events on every tracking outcome (found/notfound/error/timeout) and on WhatsApp clicks, ready for GTM triggers to pipe to GA4 / pixels.

nodes.label: Hosting consolidated to Cloudflare Pages
nodes.rationale: Netlify and Vercel config files removed to end root-repo ambiguity; the site deploys exclusively on Cloudflare Pages.

nodes.label: Client-side anti-abuse on tracking forms
nodes.rationale: Minimum interval between submits, per-minute cap, and a honeypot field to blunt mass/bot submissions on top of the worker's per-IP rate limit; plus a 12s AbortController timeout so a hung worker never traps the visitor.

nodes.label: CSP hardening (remove unsafe-inline)
nodes.rationale: unsafe-inline removed from script-src and hash-based CSP3 adopted (which silently broke Astro hydration since v2.2.0 until header+meta CSP were coordinated); inline style attributes refactored to Tailwind classes.

nodes.label: Astro 6 + Preact island stack
nodes.rationale: Site is 90% static; serving plain HTML and shipping JS only for islands (header, tracking, FAQ) sharply lowers TTI on 3G/LTE, the real scenario for many Nicaraguan users. Preact keeps the interactive slice ~15x lighter than React (3kB vs 45kB).

nodes.label: Coordinated CSP (header + meta hashes)
nodes.rationale: Header CSP (public/_headers) is permissive on inline; the Astro-generated meta CSP with per-script hashes is the bottleneck, so an injected XSS script matches no hash and is blocked. This preserves the unsafe-inline removal from commit bca07d9 without breaking Astro hydration. Devs must avoid inline style= attributes.

nodes.label: GTM snippet SHA-256 hash-pinned
nodes.rationale: The GTM snippet in Layout.astro is pinned by SHA-256 hash in astro.config.mjs; any reformat of that line changes the hash and breaks CSP, hence a prettier-ignore above the script. Changing the ID requires recomputing the hash.

nodes.label: Pre-render dark mode init script
nodes.rationale: /scripts/dark-mode-init.js loads in <head> before any other JS to avoid the light->dark flash; served unbundled from public/. Bundling or moving it brings the flash back.

nodes.label: Aggressive cache headers
nodes.rationale: Hashed assets (js/css/woff2) get max-age=31536000, immutable; index.html gets no-cache. Configured in public/_headers.

nodes.label: Mandatory Miami address flow
nodes.rationale: The client always ships to the Miami address, never Nicaragua directly; the flow is always client -> Miami -> Nicaragua and must be explicit in copy, the how-it-works steps, and tutorials. A business decision that often shows up disguised as UX.

nodes.label: No Sundays / free national delivery rules
nodes.rationale: No Sunday operations (JSON-LD LocalBusiness openingHours covers Mon-Fri); national delivery is free and included in freight, not an upsell; B2B credit 7/15 days is volume-dependent and not published; no haul/unboxing content style.
nodes.norm_label: no sundays / free national delivery rules

nodes.label: GTM container GTM-K55VC9JZ
nodes.rationale: Google Tag Manager container installed with hash-based CSP; Cloudflare Web Analytics allowed alongside GA4. Container is live and emits pageviews; conversion triggers/tags still pending on the GTM side.

nodes.label: Performance budget (FCP/TTI/bundle)
nodes.rationale: Targets to defend: FCP < 1.5s, TTI < 3s, CLS < 0.1, Lighthouse mobile > 90, bundled JS < 100kB gzip, CSS < 50kB gzip. A feature that would exceed these must be reconsidered before merge.

nodes.label: Island architecture for 3G/LTE users
nodes.rationale: Many real users are on 3G/LTE in Nicaragua, so a fast time-to-interactive is a product decision, not a micro-optimization; only the header, tracking portal, and FAQ load JS.

nodes.label: Rifa aniversario rescue
nodes.rationale: Organic reach on Instagram is only 2-5% of followers, so a small-account raffle needs paid distribution, a scroll-stopping post, and daily reminders; rescued with a $20 ad plan, improved caption, organic actions, daily stories, and a live draw script.

nodes.label: $20 Meta Ads engagement plan
nodes.rationale: A raffle is boosted with the Engagement objective, not Traffic — it is cheaper and increases real comments that amplify organic reach in parallel; targeted Nicaragua 18-45, Amazon/Shein/AliExpress interests, IG+FB feed only.

nodes.label: Meta Pixel via GTM
nodes.rationale: Connects on-site behavior to IG/FB campaigns so ads do not run blind; added from inside the existing GTM container without touching code, with ViewContent/Lead events later.

nodes.label: WhatsApp Business setup
nodes.rationale: Primary conversion channel; profile, welcome/off-hours messages, and slash quick-replies (/precios, /rastrear, /tiempo) configured on +505 8208-5181 before launch.

nodes.label: Google My Business profile
nodes.rationale: Free listing appearing in Google Maps for courier/import searches; category Freight Forwarding Service, address Carretera Masaya Km 14.5, 10+ photos by Maya.

nodes.label: Email marketing with Resend
nodes.rationale: Free to 3,000 emails/month; three first flows: quote requested, casillero welcome, review request 7 days after delivery.

nodes.label: 4-type content rotation strategy
nodes.rationale: Rotate Educativo / Testimonio / Servicio / Humano posts with AI prompt templates and Canva templates; optimal Nicaragua windows 7-8AM and 7-8PM.

nodes.label: Backlog (operational source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Git-versioned, 2-week window, one owner per task; a code task closes only when the harness gate is green, verified against code and the live worker, not against the prose.

nodes.label: GTM conversion events pending
nodes.rationale: Pageviews fire but conversion events were noted as not started in the 2026-06-16 audit; the site now emits dataLayer pushes yet the GTM-side triggers/tags to consume them are still missing, so objective 1 is not yet measurable.

nodes.label: Harness L1 (partial)
nodes.rationale: ci.yml runs pnpm check on PRs for both repos, but branch protection and typecheck+lint in the gate are still missing (check today only runs tests+build).

nodes.label: HIT CARGO business model (freight forwarder)
nodes.rationale: International logistics company helping Nicaragua import from the US/China/Panama; a freight forwarder / logistics intermediary, not an online store — it does not sell its own products or hold inventory, using a Miami address as reception and consolidation hub.

nodes.label: Client -> Miami -> Nicaragua service flow
nodes.rationale: Client buys online, ships to HIT CARGO's Miami address (never Nicaragua directly), packages are received and optionally consolidated, transported to Nicaragua, cleared through customs, and delivered.

nodes.label: Services (air/sea/consolidation/customs)
nodes.rationale: Air (fast, per pound), sea (cheaper, consolidated, for heavy/bulky), Miami reception, consolidation, customs nationalization, free national delivery, and pre-purchase buying advice.

nodes.label: Not an ecommerce (restrictions)
nodes.rationale: NOT an online store, no own products, no inventory, no Shein/Amazon-style catalog, no haul/unboxing content, no Sunday work — the nuance matters because copy and UX sometimes confuse the two models.

nodes.label: B2B / enterprise credit 7-15 days
nodes.rationale: Corporate services: machinery/parts import, technical advice, formal invoicing, and 7- or 15-day credit lines depending on volume.

nodes.label: Everest scraper plan v3 (Cloudflare all-in)
nodes.rationale: Definitive strategy for an intelligent scraper + API ecosystem leveraging Cloudflare and Supabase for speed and zero server maintenance; historical vision, see hit-ever2 for what was actually implemented.

nodes.label: Cloudflare Browser Rendering scraper
nodes.rationale: Playwright via Remote Browser on Cloudflare eliminates external servers; scraping traffic exits Cloudflare's network, lowering IP-block risk and latency.

nodes.label: Hono API on Cloudflare Workers
nodes.rationale: Hono on Workers serves both /track/:id (cache-first, then trigger browser + AI clean + save) and the client system, chosen for high RPS and near-zero cold start.

nodes.label: GPT-4o-mini HTML parsing
nodes.rationale: Instead of fragile per-field CSS selectors, Playwright captures the legacy HTML table block and passes it to GPT-4o-mini to extract structured JSON.

nodes.label: Cookie vault (Upstash Redis, ASP session)
nodes.rationale: The everest.cargotrack.net session depends on ASPSESSIONID cookies stored in Upstash Redis and injected into each Cloudflare Browser session to bypass login; refreshed/rotated every 12-15 minutes.

nodes.label: Supabase persistence + client system
nodes.rationale: Supabase is the history store and the core of the client system: Auth for users/companies, Postgres tables for shipments and a points/pounds ledger, automated backups.

nodes.label: Everest cargotrack (Classic ASP source)
nodes.rationale: everest.cargotrack.net is a Classic ASP system with old but consistent HTML; warehouse numbers extracted from td/table selectors, session held via ASPSESSIONID cookies.

nodes.label: Landing image / visual asset plan
nodes.rationale: The SEO+copy audit rewrites all copy, but the visual bank is insufficient to back it; images are both SEO (alt/OG/rich results) and conversion (proof, trust, differentiation).

nodes.label: Miami is reseller, no own warehouse
nodes.rationale: The Miami service is resale of a larger agent — HIT CARGO has no own warehouse. Copy must switch 'bodega propia en Miami' to neutral, true 'casillero/tu direccion en Miami'; the big agent's photos are not used publicly to avoid revealing resale; substitute an SVG route map.
nodes.norm_label: miami is reseller, no own warehouse

nodes.label: Remove fake testimonials + aggregateRating
nodes.rationale: Stock testimonials and a fake aggregateRating (4.9/342) are removed from the DOM and JSON-LD (not just hidden) to avoid a Google manual penalty, replaced with verifiable aggregate figures and a store-logo band as indirect social proof.

nodes.label: Own photography for credibility
nodes.rationale: Team/office, the real Nicaragua-visible process, and door delivery must be real (not stock/generated) to back promises; a half-day modern-phone shoot suffices, Maya as owner.

nodes.label: Hero image optimization (Core Web Vitals)
nodes.rationale: The 2.9MB / 4118x2694 hero JPEG makes LCP < 2.5s impossible; compress to <=250KB webp with 3 responsive sizes via astro:assets.

nodes.label: Master Plan 2026-2028 (canonical roadmap)
nodes.rationale: Canonical business roadmap for a part-time family team (~10-15h/week): make HIT CARGO Nicaragua's import leader via intelligent automation, phased quick-wins through escalation.

nodes.label: Phase 1 quick wins
nodes.rationale: Week 1-2, zero investment, immediate ROI: dynamic pricing variables, WhatsApp Business automation, and a Google My Business profile.

nodes.label: business.config.ts dynamic variables
nodes.rationale: A single business.config.ts holds all prices, promotions, and stats so Maya or Abi can update prices site-wide by editing one file with zero programming.

nodes.label: Phase 2 automation base
nodes.rationale: Month 1-2: email automation with Resend, optimized Meta Pixel + Conversions API, and a Tidio FAQ chatbot to offload repetitive replies.

nodes.label: Phase 4 tracking MVP (realistic scraper)
nodes.rationale: A deliberately simplified scraper (Cloudflare Worker every 6h, Supabase free tier, 2 Hono endpoints, simple Preact frontend, regex parsing, no AI for now) is the unique differentiator competitors lack.

nodes.label: Referral + points loyalty program
nodes.rationale: Revenue optimization via automated upsells, a simple referral code program, and a points/tiers (Bronce/Plata/Oro) system to lift AOV and repeat rate.

nodes.label: Anti-burnout rules (part-time)
nodes.rationale: Part-time guardrails: Renato max ~4h/week and one feature at a time, no fixed public deadlines, batch creation for Maya/Abi; family over business, progress over perfection.

nodes.label: Growth KPIs / metrics targets
nodes.rationale: Monthly KPIs (visitors, conversion, clients, ticket, recurrence, CAC/LTV, revenue) plus weekly secondaries; reviewed in a 30-minute biweekly meeting per role.

nodes.label: AI multi-agent workflow guide
nodes.rationale: Portable design aligned with Anthropic and OpenAI agent guidance; complements the delivery harness — the harness is verification, this doc is how the verified work is produced.

nodes.label: Agent produces, harness verifies
nodes.rationale: Nothing merges because a capable model wrote it — only because the gate is green, exactly like human code; this reframes 'do I trust this model?' into 'does my harness catch errors anyone could make?', the question that scales. Step 4 (pnpm check: types/lint/tests/build) is the hinge.

nodes.label: Agent roles (explore/plan/build/review)
nodes.rationale: Explorer (read-only locate), Planner (strategy before code), Builder (bounded 1-2 file edits), and adversarial Reviewer, mapped to Claude Code subagents; read-only subagents investigate without spending main-thread context.

nodes.label: Start simple, escalate only when justified
nodes.rationale: A well-contexted prompt beats an agent, an agent beats a multi-agent orchestra; raise complexity only when the result justifies it, since each level adds latency, cost, and failure modes. Multi-agent pays off only for parallel, independent work.
nodes.norm_label: start simple, escalate only when justified

nodes.label: feat -> staging -> master branch flow
nodes.rationale: master is production, staging is pre-production testing, feat/* are features; changes flow via PRs (feat->staging, staging->master), tagged with semantic versions for easy rollback.

nodes.label: Cloudflare Pages auto-deploy on master
nodes.rationale: Push to master triggers a Cloudflare Pages production build (pnpm build -> dist); feature branches get preview deploys at <branch>.<project>.pages.dev, with a custom domain and Full (strict) SSL.

nodes.label: Deprecated Netlify/Vercel configs
nodes.rationale: netlify.toml and vercel.json archived in 2026-06 during hosting consolidation; they created root-repo ambiguity and their script-src 'self' (no unsafe-inline, no GTM domain) would break GTM and Astro hydration if active. The current CSP lives in public/_headers + astro.config.mjs.

nodes.label: Master Plan 2025 (legacy, superseded)
nodes.rationale: Superseded by the 2024-2026 master plan; do not use its fixed 2025 dates. An ambitious 6-phase technical roadmap (Astro5 migration through regional/blockchain expansion).

nodes.label: Astro 5 + Preact migration (v2.0)
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 (completed): migrated React SPA to Astro 5 + Preact, zero-JS by default, island architecture, dark mode, Vitest; TTI cut ~90% on 3G/LTE.

nodes.label: AI tracking system phase (planned)
nodes.rationale: Ambitious Phase 2: Cloudflare Browser Rendering + Workers + Upstash Redis cookie vault + Supabase + GPT-4o-mini parsing of Everest's Classic ASP, an enterprise client portal and points system — later scoped down to the realistic tracking MVP.

nodes.label: React SPA -> Astro migration plan
nodes.rationale: The React SPA added ~150KB JS before first paint, indexed worse for SEO, and showed a blank screen on intermittent 3G/LTE (high bounce risk); migrating to Astro 5 + Preact 10 cut framework JS ~90%, standardized on pnpm, made Hero/Footer/Services 0-JS, and moved to clean URLs.

nodes.label: Everest Scraper & API Plan v3 (Cloudflare All-in)
nodes.rationale: Legacy plan superseded by hit-ever2; uses Supabase and Cloudflare Browser Rendering, not the current InsForge stack.
nodes.norm_label: everest scraper & api plan v3 (cloudflare all-in)

nodes.label: Cloudflare Browser Rendering
nodes.rationale: Scraping traffic exits Cloudflare's network, reducing IP-block risk and latency and eliminating external servers (Railway/Vercel).

nodes.label: Cookie Vault (Upstash Redis, ASPSESSIONID)
nodes.rationale: Store Everest ASP session cookies in Redis and inject them into browser sessions to bypass login.

nodes.label: AI parsing with GPT-4o-mini
nodes.rationale: Pass the captured Almacen table block to the LLM to extract structured JSON instead of maintaining fragile CSS selectors.

nodes.label: Silent background processing via Cron Triggers
nodes.rationale: Process new shipments in the background so data is ready before the client queries it.
nodes.norm_label: silent background processing via cron triggers

nodes.label: HIT CARGO Brand Book (Manual de marca)
nodes.rationale: Canonical source of truth for brand; tailwind.config.js implements it.
nodes.norm_label: hit cargo brand book (manual de marca)

nodes.label: Naranja HIT #FF7A00 (protagonista)
nodes.rationale: The 10% accent that marks action, focus and CTAs; señala, no decora. Never tint an entire photo orange.

nodes.label: Azul Navy #14213D (soporte estratégico)
nodes.rationale: Reinforces trust and professionalism; it is base, never an accent or CTA.

nodes.label: Proporcion 70/20/10
nodes.rationale: 70% dark base, 20% white breathing room, 10% Naranja HIT protagonist.

nodes.label: Tipografia Montserrat + Poppins
nodes.rationale: Consistency achieved by weight, not by mixing fonts; no italics or scripts.

nodes.label: Estilo fotografico (dir. de arte, §07)
nodes.rationale: Real, warm, dynamic images with real people and cargo; never cold or generic stock.

nodes.label: Data-driven content architecture (src/content, src/config)
nodes.rationale: Content separated from presentation so the team edits text/prices/services without touching markup.

nodes.label: Tracker: estandarizacion Everest + Global Connection
nodes.rationale: Standardize raw provider logs to clear milestones; orange only for the current step; never invent dates.

nodes.label: Copy, SEO & Conversion Audit v3.0
nodes.rationale: Based on real code; goal is #1 ranking in Nicaragua for import/courier searches.

nodes.label: FAQPage schema
nodes.rationale: Enables FAQ accordions directly in Google SERP, doubling visual space.

nodes.label: aggregateRating (must be real)
nodes.rationale: Enables star rich snippets but a fake rating triggers a Google manual penalty; verify reviewCount before publishing.

nodes.label: Voz de marca / vos nicaraguense
nodes.rationale: Concrete over abstract, benefit before description, numbers when they exist, no corporate jargon.

nodes.label: Google My Business + NAP consistency
nodes.rationale: Name, address and phone must be identical across every channel for local SEO.

nodes.label: Phone correction to +50582085181
nodes.rationale: JSON-LD carried a wrong number (+50578819018); must match the business number everywhere.

nodes.label: Pagina /precios faltante
nodes.rationale: Absence of a public pricing page is the site's biggest conversion loss; users searching cost find competitors.

nodes.label: Photo Direction & Replacement Guide
nodes.rationale: Decides which photo goes where and how it must look so replacement/optimization is mechanical when assets arrive.

nodes.label: Performance budget (hero LCP <=200KB)
nodes.rationale: FCP < 1.5s, LCP < 2.5s mobile, Lighthouse > 90; the hero is risk #1, hence the 200KB cap.

nodes.label: astro:assets / sharp optimization
nodes.rationale: Generates srcset + AVIF/WebP + LQIP automatically; never upload uncompressed camera JPGs.

nodes.label: Prueba social sin retratos de stock
nodes.rationale: Fake stock portraits hurt credibility; use verifiable aggregate figures plus a band of store logos instead.
nodes.norm_label: prueba social sin retratos de stock

nodes.label: Vara logistica top mundial (Maersk/DHL/Flexport)
nodes.rationale: Real operation at scale, people at the center, movement, restraint: one strong image per section.

nodes.label: T&C Rifa de Aniversario HIT CARGO 2026
nodes.norm_label: t&c rifa de aniversario hit cargo 2026

nodes.label: Premio: 2 creditos de envio USD $15
nodes.rationale: Non-cash, non-transferable credit valid 60 days for one shipment.

nodes.label: Elegibilidad y forma de participar
nodes.rationale: 18+, Nicaragua residents, follow/like/tag; max 3 entries; random selection tool on live stream.

nodes.label: Independencia de Meta/Instagram/Facebook
nodes.rationale: Promotion is not sponsored or associated with Meta; participants release Meta from liability.

nodes.label: Technical & Process Audit (June 2026)
nodes.rationale: Snapshot: code is well built but has no automated safety net between commit and production.

nodes.label: Falta de harness / red de seguridad automatizada
nodes.rationale: Zero CI, one test file, no typecheck in pipeline, no pre/post-deploy validation; every deploy is an act of faith.
nodes.norm_label: falta de harness / red de seguridad automatizada

nodes.label: Tracking 100% mock (luego resuelto)
nodes.rationale: At cut-off the tracker was a hardcoded stub; the post-cut update notes it was connected to hit-ever2 via fetch /track/:guia.

nodes.label: GTM sin eventos de conversion
nodes.rationale: GTM-K55VC9JZ installed but zero dataLayer.push, so objective 1 (conversion) is not measurable.

nodes.label: Cloudflare Pages auto-deploy (push master)
nodes.rationale: Push to master triggers build+deploy in ~2 min; every branch gets a preview URL; no GitHub Actions needed for deploy.

nodes.label: Env vars (PUBLIC_API_URL, PUBLIC_ prefix)
nodes.rationale: Never hardcode secrets; PUBLIC_ prefix exposes to client, no prefix for server secrets.

nodes.label: Static mode vs SSR decision
nodes.rationale: The site is 100% static; no @astrojs/cloudflare adapter or Workers needed. Remove the wrangler deploy command that forced SSR.

nodes.label: KV namespace collision (code 10014)
nodes.rationale: A custom 'npx wrangler deploy' deploy command made Wrangler create Worker/KV resources, colliding on an existing namespace.

nodes.label: La confianza vive en el gate (principio rector)
nodes.rationale: Decouples quality from any individual or LLM: whoever produces the change, it passes the same checks or does not reach prod.
nodes.norm_label: la confianza vive en el gate (principio rector)

nodes.label: Fases F0-F6 del harness
nodes.rationale: Deterministic, unavoidable, and fast where it matters: cheap local checks first, complete CI later.

nodes.label: pnpm check (gate completo en un comando)
nodes.rationale: typecheck + lint + test + build; same contract runs locally (F1/F2) and in CI (F3) because gates call npm scripts, not embedded CI logic.
nodes.norm_label: pnpm check (gate completo en un comando)

nodes.label: Niveles de madurez L0-L3
nodes.rationale: A part-time team levels up only when the previous level is stable; L1 gate-of-merge is the immediate objective.

nodes.label: Proteccion de rama (CI verde + 1 review)
nodes.rationale: Makes the gate non-bypassable: master cannot be merged without CI green plus one review.

nodes.label: GitFlow (feature -> staging -> master)
nodes.rationale: Recommended flow: test in staging, review before prod, easy rollback, clean history, CI/CD compatible.

nodes.label: Security & Correctness Audit (July 2026)
nodes.rationale: No exploitable critical/high vulnerability; findings are hardening plus one real tracker UX bug.

nodes.label: CSP header ∩ meta intersection
nodes.rationale: The meta script-src has no 'unsafe-inline', only hashes, so an injected inline script matches nothing and is blocked.

nodes.label: HSTS fix (estaba faltando)
nodes.rationale: HSTS was documented as present but missing from _headers; Cloudflare Pages does not add it automatically. Added without preload.

nodes.label: Tracker abort/analytics ghost bug fix
nodes.rationale: The catch treated every abort as failure, reopening an error overlay and pushing a false track_search/timeout to GTM.

nodes.label: Cloudflare hardening (WAF, Bot Fight, Turnstile, Rate Limiting)
nodes.norm_label: cloudflare hardening (waf, bot fight, turnstile, rate limiting)

nodes.label: Correccion de la afirmacion '0 vulnerabilidades'
nodes.rationale: pnpm audit reports ~22 advisories; the security-deployment-guide claim of 0 vulns / 8.5-9 score is no longer true.

nodes.label: Puntuacion de seguridad 8.5/10 (disputada)
nodes.rationale: Claims 0 vulnerabilities and 8.5-9/10; later contradicted by the July 2026 audit.

nodes.label: Roles: Explore/Plan/Build/Verify/Review/Ship
nodes.rationale: A narrow role per phase with fresh context yields better results and less error margin; each agent does one phase and stops.

nodes.label: Estado en artefacto (.feature/<slug>.md)
nodes.rationale: State lives in a shared file, not agent memory, so any agent/person can pick up the next phase; no agent is a single point of failure.

nodes.label: Verify = el harness (pnpm check verde)
nodes.rationale: Never skip step 4: Build without Verify is 'I think it works', with Verify the gate confirms it.

nodes.label: robots.txt (Allow all + sitemap)
nodes.rationale: Allows all crawlers and points to https://hit-cargo.com/sitemap-index.xml.

nodes.label: HIT Cargo full logo lockup (dark variant)
nodes.norm_label: hit cargo full logo lockup (dark variant)

nodes.label: HIT Cargo full logo lockup (globe + arrow isotype with HIT CARGO wordmark)
nodes.norm_label: hit cargo full logo lockup (globe + arrow isotype with hit cargo wordmark)

nodes.label: HIT Cargo isotype mark (orange globe + upward arrow)
nodes.norm_label: hit cargo isotype mark (orange globe + upward arrow)

nodes.label: HIT Cargo Open Graph social-share image (branded plane + truck airport scene)
nodes.norm_label: hit cargo open graph social-share image (branded plane + truck airport scene)

hyperedges.label: Tracking system across plan and implementation
CSP hardening across headers, meta, and hosting configs
